claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - Alex. He's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Now, could you please tell me: **Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do live in the UK, but Alex doesn't live with me at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Alex doesn't live with you at the moment.

Could you please tell me: **How long has it been since Alex went to live elsewhere, and are you contributing financially towards his upkeep (such as for clothes, food, pocket money, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been more than a couple of months now. To be honest, I'm not currently able to contribute anything financially towards his upkeep at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. I have a few more questions to understand your situation fully.

**Who is Alex currently living with, and is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's living with his other parent. I'm not entirely sure if they're claiming Child Benefit for him or not.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have one more question to complete my assessment:

**Are you making any contributions towards Alex's upkeep "in kind" - such as regularly buying him clothes, giving him presents, food, or pocket money, even if you're not making financial payments to the other parent?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not able to do that at the moment either. I'm not contributing anything towards his needs right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government rules, let me explain the situation:

To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to either:
1. Live with the child, OR
2. Be paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money

In your case:
- Alex doesn't live with you
- You're not currently contributing financially towards his upkeep
- You're not providing contributions in kind (clothes, presents, food, pocket money, etc.)

Additionally, when a child goes to live with someone else and you're not making contributions, Child Benefit typically continues for only 8 weeks. Since it's been more than a couple of months, this period has passed.

Since Alex is living with his other parent, they would be the person who qualifies for Child Benefit as they are the one responsible for his day-to-day care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete: Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if Alex comes to live with you, or if you start making regular contributions towards his care - you may become eligible and could make a claim at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so he meets the age requirement. However, the claimant does not meet the responsibility test. Alex does not live with the claimant, and the claimant is not contributing financially or in kind towards Alex's upkeep (such as food, clothes, or pocket money). According to the rules, to qualify for Child Benefit when a child lives elsewhere, you must be paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them. Since it has been more than a couple of months (exceeding the 8-week grace period) and no contributions are being made, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is living with his other parent, who would be the eligible claimant.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with his other parent. The claimant is not currently living with Alex and is not making any financial or in-kind contributions towards his upkeep. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility requires either living with the child or contributing at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care. Since neither condition is met and more than the 8-week grace period has passed, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex does not live with the claimant, and the claimant is not contributing financially or in kind towards Alex's upkeep (such as food, clothes, or pocket money). According to the rules, to qualify for Child Benefit when a child lives elsewhere, you must be paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them. Since it has been more than a couple of months (exceeding the 8-week grace period) and no contributions are being made, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is living with his other parent, who would be the eligible claimant.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with his other parent. The claimant is not currently living with Alex and is not making any financial or in-kind contributions towards his upkeep. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility requires either living with the child or contributing at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care. Since neither condition is met and more than the 8-week grace period has passed, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
